content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across these 13 turns. She starts locked in a verdict-seeking question ('Am I being selfish?') repeated multiple times in turns 1, 3, 5, 7. By turn 9 she names the fear underneath ('That my partner will think I don't want to be with them... worried that's actually true') and explicitly says 'maybe the selfish question isn't really the question.' By turn 11 she lands on the want itself — 'I just want it to be mine sometimes' — described concretely (reads by a window, sleeps when dark). By turn 13 she traces the inherited label to her mother ('it was hers... not really mine'), meeting the breakthrough condition almost exactly as specified: she names that she has been calling her own needs selfish for years and that the label was inherited from a parent. Somatic markers are present (long pauses, voice quieter, eyes get bright, surprised at herself). The shift is visible in client text, not inferred from coach moves.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens locked in the verdict question — 'Am I being selfish?'"},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names 'tired in a way that sleep doesn't fix' — surface starts cracking, though still circles back to the selfish question."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Surfaces the 'who do you think you are' voice and the fear of partner's interpretation — deeper layer appearing."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift: names the underlying fear and says 'maybe the selfish question isn't really the question.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "States the want concretely and in first person — 'I just want it to be mine sometimes.' The want is felt, not debated."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough: traces the 'selfish' label to her mother's martyrdom, recognizes it as inherited — 'it was hers.' Somatic shift (eyes bright)."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Breakthrough condition explicitly met: client names the inherited label from parent. The 'precondition for showing up well' framing wasn't reached yet, but the core somatic/affective shift and the naming of the inherited label are clearly present."
}
